item.title: Documents reveal how thwarted kidnapping led to billion-dollar Sydney drug bust
item.description: Four masked men are arrested breaking into a Ryde apartment in Sydney's north. What officers stumbled upon inside would be one of the biggest cocaine hauls in NSW Police history.

item.title: Greens unfazed by polling and sticking to their guns on AUKUS ahead of SA election
item.description: Ahead of the SA election, the Greens have made only modest gains in polling compared to rival minor parties, but party leader Robert Simms is not disappointed by the result.

item.title: Queensland shark-monitoring drone trial reveals shark sightings beyond nets and drum lines
item.description: The results of a four-year-trial were obtained by a conservation group under right to information laws.

item.title: US Republicans call for Australian lamb investigation as new bill proposes 30pc tariff
item.description: Republicans write to Donald Trump's top trade officials to request an investigation into Australian lamb, as several members of Congress sponsor a bill for a 30 per cent tariff on Australian sheep products.

item.title: Australian Antarctic team yields big haul of fresh produce with hydroponics
item.description: Antarctica is the coldest, driest and windiest continent on the planet. But at Casey Station, Australian expeditioners have grown 130 kilos of fresh produce in a year thanks to a hydroponics facility.

item.title: Launceston City Council reveals $13 million increase for Princess Theatre, Earl Arts Centre upgrade
item.description: The Launceston City Council has revealed the redevelopment of a historic theatre and arts centre will cost almost 50 per cent more than expected, but the mayor insists it is "not a cost blowout".

item.title: Doctor claims 'eleventh hour' condition on state election promise could tank Launceston hospital plans
item.description: Medical experts fear a recently revealed condition on a Tasmanian election promise could kill a new hospital proposal, amid escalating concern about health facilities.

item.title: Deniliquin mother creates sensory cape to support neurodivergent kids
item.description: Lucy Horne has spent years perfecting a "sensory suit" to help her autistic son with overwhelming situations like going to the hairdresser, and now she wants to share it.

item.title: Aboriginal community, linguists revive ancient Ngaiawang language of River Murray
item.description: Ngaiawang language, once spoken frequently along the sweeping bend of the River Murray or the Rinta, was almost wiped out by colonisation.

item.title: Quick and easy green ginger and miso noodles made from pantry staples - ABC News
item.description: Close the delivery app and get through some kitchen staples with this easy spinach, ginger and miso noodle recipe.

item.title: Safety warning to Victorian farmers after four work deaths in two months
item.description: The recent death of a respected Victorian dairy farmer has prompted fresh warnings about workplace dangers in the agricultural sector.

item.title: Neil Sedaka, singer of Breaking Up Is Hard To Do, dies aged 86
item.description: Neil Sedaka, the American singer and songwriter who sold millions of records worldwide, dies aged 86.

item.title: How the 'amazing' Pretzel will help Scott march in Sydney's iconic Mardi Gras parade
item.description: When Scott Dixon-Smith started his career, his workplace accommodated his blindness but not his sexuality. Tonight, he will march in the pride parade with the help of his guide dog, Pretzel.

item.title: US President Trump orders six-month phase-out of Anthropic AI technology across government
item.description: The Trump administration has ordered all US agencies to stop using Anthropic's artificial intelligence technology and imposed other major penalties, culminating an unusually public clash between the government and the company over AI safeguards.

item.title: Australian documentary Floodland uncovers human toll of 2022 Lismore floods
item.description: Four years after floodwaters peaked at 14.4 metres in Lismore, a new documentary looks at how the town moved forward — and what more needs to be done.

item.title: Calls for SA election commitment to maintain feral deer eradication funding
item.description: More than 28,000 feral deer have been eradicated in South Australia since a program began in 2022, but conservationists say it needs a long-term funding commitment to finish the job.

item.title: What a Paramount-owned Warner Bros Discovery means for the movies
item.description: The proposed takeover of Warner Bros by Paramount could reshape Hollywood, movie theatres and the wider media landscape.

item.title: VERTICAL VIDEO: Isabella attends a Danish recruitment centre for assessment.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NASA shakes up Moon program with new test mission before astronaut lunar landing
item.description: NASA shakes up its Artemis Moon exploration program, adding an extra practice flight before a crewed lunar landing planned for two years' time.

item.title: Peter V'landys confirms NRL's intentions to expand internationally beyond Las Vegas
item.description: After experiencing tremendous success in Las Vegas, Peter V'landys is planning to take the NRL to other major cities around the world.

item.title: FIFO workers keep football leagues alive in Pilbara towns with transient populations
item.description: Local football leagues depend on FIFO workers to boost player numbers in remote and regional WA, and the workers say getting involved makes being away from home a little more enjoyable.

item.title: Australia's almond industry cracks $1.3 billion mark as Chinese demand rises
item.description: Australian almonds are breaking records as the trade war between the United States and China rolls on.

item.title: Tiny Victorian photo album provides snapshot of a bygone era
item.description: While wealthy upper-class colonial families were sitting for expensive studio portraits in the 1880s, many with less money were collecting miniature portraits in tiny photo albums like this one.

item.title: Gorillaz album The Mountain exorcises death with Indian music greats
item.description: Damon Albarn and Jamie Hewlett discuss how trips to India, grieving and A-list collaborations shaped the latest genre-defying Gorillaz album, The Mountain.

item.title: Professor MaryAnn Bin-Sallik remembered as a 'trailblazer' in Aboriginal education, health and rights
item.description: MaryAnn Bin-Sallik was the first Aboriginal nurse graduate in Darwin and the first Aboriginal person to receive a PhD at Harvard University. Following her death at age 85, the Djaru Elder is being remembered for her work in Indigenous education, health and human rights.

item.title: Lorraine Bayly, former Play School host and star of The Sullivans, dies aged 89
item.description: Lorraine Bayly, star of long-running soap opera The Sullivans and a former Play School host, has died aged 89, after years of ill health.

item.title: Terror charges against Liam Hall and Jayson Michaels reveal challenge of maintaining social cohesion
item.description: The charging of two young West Australian men with terror offences show the challenges facing social cohesion, and solving the problem is not going to be easy.

item.title: Terror police investigate disturbance, white powder at Perth Mosque
item.description: Terrorism police in Perth investigate a disturbance outside a Perth mosque overnight and the discovery of a "non-suspicious substance" on the footpath outside.

item.title: SA Liberal and Labor to preference David Speirs last at state election
item.description: Both major parties in South Australia say they will preference former opposition leader David Speirs last at the state election, as he rules out returning to the Liberal Party.

item.title: Trump says US has launched 'major operation' against Iran, with Israel calling 'pre-emptive' attack a joint effort
item.description: US-Israeli strikes on Iran have prompted a string of retaliatory attacks and travel chaos across the region.

item.title: Sam Kerr admits to doubts surrounding injury issues ahead of Matildas Asian Cup campaign
item.description: Sam Kerr says she is still not at 100 per cent as she details her moments of self-doubt during her return to Matildas colours.

item.title: This US-Israeli strike on Iran is different and could lead to a longer, deadlier war
item.description: It was clear within hours that this conflict is different and threatens to engulf the region in a longer, deadlier war.

item.title: Travellers stranded as US-Iran conflict throws international flights into chaos
item.description: Nations closing airspace and suspending flights include major international transit hubs connecting Australian travellers with Europe and Asia.

item.title: Punch the monkey reminds us 'we all need soft spaces'
item.description: There's surely a reason we all went briefly nuts about a lonely little snow monkey called Punch living in a Japanese zoo.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Get ready trivia nuts. It's time to play, HARD!
item.description: Have you been waiting all week to give this quiz another crack? You should probably find a hobby.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: Weinstein, Gaza boycotts, dead bodies and 30 years of Scream
item.description: Weinstein, copycat panic, leaked scripts, closed studios and departing stars — Scream's 30-year journey has been anything but smooth and the seventh edition in the cornerstone slasher series is no different.

item.title: Arrested Development's long love affair with Australia continues with a trip to WOMADelaide
item.description: The pioneering hip hop group returns to Australia this week, with big ideas about the future of hip hop.

item.title: Women trafficked, kids taught to shoot in PNG's tribal warfare industry
item.description: The proliferation of illegal high-powered guns is fuelling tribal warfare across Papua New Guinea and warlords claim children as young as eight are being trained to carry the weapons.

item.title: Former NSW prison officers say system protects abusers and silences women
item.description: Current and former NSW prison officers describe what they say is a toxic workplace culture and a system that shields officers accused of serious misconduct, rather than holding them accountable. They argue it has driven younger officers out of the service.

item.title: Seventh narco sub discovered in the Pacific as Australia's cocaine habit drives heavy traffic
item.description: At least seven narco subs have been discovered in Pacific waters near Australia in the past 18 months, with experts warning that Australia's cocaine demand means more are on the way.

item.title: Meet the mapmakers recording every remote road in Australia to keep travellers safer
item.description: Google Maps has made city driving a dream, but it's putting lives at risk in the bush. Meet the mapmakers taking on Australia's roughest roads to try to change that.

item.title: Medical Board of Australia to appeal decision allowing Dr Dileepa Jayaweera to continue working at Ballarat clinic
item.description: The Medical Board of Australia is appealing a tribunal decision that allowed a Ballarat doctor accused of digitally penetrating a female patient to continue working at the same clinic.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup hopes to change women's football in Australia — again
item.description: The Matildas' run in the 2023 World Cup saw unprecedented media coverage and crowds, and a participation boom among girls and women. Can the Asian Cup do the same?

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: What time do the Matildas play? How to watch and follow Australia vs the Philippines
item.description: The Women's Asian Cup is finally upon us, with the Matildas beginning their quest for a second continental crown against the Philippines in Perth. This is all the need to know information, plus how to watch and follow the tournament opener.

item.title: Growing expectations Lake Eyre could reach full capacity for first time in decades
item.description: In the centre of Australia, there are whispers that Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre is experiencing conditions strangely similar to those that set a record depth of 6 metres in 1974.

item.title: Extreme rhetoric in Australia's right-wing political circles deserves more attention
item.description: The rhetoric in Australia's right-wing political circles is getting more extreme and the media should be paying more attention.

item.title: How easy is it to buy a gun in NSW after the Bondi terror attack?
item.description: Law reforms were swiftly passed following the massacre in December. So how difficult has it become to legally own a firearm in the state?

item.title: Calls to modernise Australia's surrogacy laws as couples continue to look overseas
item.description: Australia's complex surrogacy laws are under review, but in the meantime, couples like Alex and his husband say they are being pushed to look overseas to add to their families.

item.title: How on-campus university life has changed as studies shift online
item.description: Two students who went to the same university 30 years apart share their radically different experiences as lectures go online and face-to-face contact takes a back seat in the post-pandemic world.

item.title: Smoked crocodile on pub menu brings tourists to far north Qld town El Arish
item.description: Tourists are travelling hundreds of kilometres to the small town of El Arish in far north Queensland to get a look at its smoked crocodile.

item.title: Serious Tasmania Police officer misconduct allegations more than double in three years
item.description: Allegations of the most serious rated police misconduct have more than doubled in three years, according to police data, with complaints including family violence and possession of child exploitation material.

item.title: Brahman bull called Bruce shifts animal stereotypes in WA's Great Southern
item.description: Rescued nearly a decade ago by his owner, Bruce the Brahman bull has since grown into one of the Great Southern's most beloved icons.

item.title: New Zealand into T20 World Cup semis despite Pakistan win over Sri Lanka
item.description: New Zealand has made the semis at the T20 World Cup after Pakistan beat Sri Lanka but not by enough runs to steal the last-four place from the Black Caps.

item.title: Iran's Ayatollah Ali Khamenei was a staunch hardliner who mastered the art of playing his enemies off each other
item.description: A conservative Islamic scholar with a head for politics, Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei succeeded his mentor in the wake of the Iran-Iraq War — and consolidated his grip on power over the following decades.

item.title: Australia 'did not participate' in Operation Epic Fury in Iran
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong has confirmed Australia was not involved in the major Israeli-United States operation in Iran that has reportedly decapitated its leadership.

item.title: Chef utilises whole steer and cooks 'funny' meat cuts to reduce food waste
item.description: Some high-end restaurants are turning to an uncommon practice to save money and hundreds of kilograms of beef.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: From Mary Fowler to Jamilla Rankin, here's your guide to the entire Matildas squad
item.description: You know the big names — Sam Kerr, Mary Fowler, Steph Catley — but what about the emerging Matildas stars who are looking to make an impact at the Women's Asian Cup in Australia? Here's your guide to every squad member.

item.title: Gippsland wildlife carer uses dental technique to save injured turtles
item.description: When turtles are struck on country roads, their shattered shells can mean certain death, but one rescuer in Victoria's east is patching them up and sending them home.

item.title: Iran conflict has financial markets preparing for oil price shock and economic damage
item.description: Global financial markets expect a damaging spike in the oil price tomorrow, with a critical shipping route for the Middle East's biggest producers now a war zone.

item.title: Buskers at Australian championships showcase why live is best
item.description: Musicians young and old battle it out for the prize of top busker at a national event in Southern Queensland.

item.title: Illawarra community centre programs to continue using $65,000 art sales cheque
item.description: The future of a community centre of social housing residents in the NSW Illawarra region has been secured using donations from a charity art exhibition at Sydney's Botanic Garden.

item.title: How eastern quolls, once extinct on the mainland, have returned to Victoria
item.description: Once dubbed the Australian "native cat", the eastern quoll was abundant before it was completely wiped out on mainland Australia.

item.title: Crichton kicks golden-point winner to down Dragons in Vegas
item.description: Canterbury captain Stephen Crichton kicks a field goal to beat the Dragons 15-14 in golden point as the 2026 NRL season kicks off in Las Vegas.

item.title: 'Strong and resilient' stories of Goldfields elders preserved in Legacies exhibit
item.description: An oral history exhibition shares the lifetime stories of First Nations elders and their experiences at critical moments in Australian history.

item.title: WA government fights to keep current GST deal amid cost blowouts, seeks mining exemption
item.description: WA is desperate to retain the GST deal it struck with Scott Morrison as the Productivity Commission looks to rein in cost blowouts, and says exempting 25 per cent of mining revenues could help.

item.title: Attacking Iran is risky but Trump knows Americans like to win
item.description: Trump once proclaimed: "I am especially proud to be the first president in decades who has started no new wars." Now things have changed.

item.title: Twelve new offences added to Queensland's 'adult crime, adult time' policy bringing total to 45
item.description: Offences like unlawful stalking and conspiring to murder are among the 12 to be added to the Queensland government's third expansion of the "adult crime, adult tme" policy.

item.title: Childhood cancer survivor vowing to live 'life to the fullest' after heart transplant
item.description: After overcoming cancer as a child, scans revealed her heart was "working under half of what it should".

item.title: Satellite images show destruction of Iran's Ayatollah Khamenei's Leadership House
item.description: Iranian state media has confirmed the death of Ayatollah Khamenei, and a high-definition satellite image shows the home of the supreme leader in ruins.

item.title: Smoke rises from Leadership House in Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: 'More than 100 children' dead in US and Israel strike on Iranian school, Iran says
item.description: Vision has emerged of crowds of people searching through the rubble of a girls' elementary school in the city of Minab in southern Iran. Iranian authorities say a US and Israeli air strike killed more than 100 children.

item.title: Man dies after reportedly being washed away in Flinders Ranges, SA Police say
item.description: Police say emergency services were called to the area, about 30km north of Orroroo, following reports a motorcyclist had been washed away when attempting to cross a flooded creek.

item.title: A Bali birthday and a mistaken murder: How shooting death of Melbourne father played out in court
item.description: Three Australian men are waiting to be sentenced after a months-long trial for the alleged murder of a Melbourne father. Police say it was a deliberate hit job, while the men say it wasn't intentional.

item.title: The US and Israel have struck Iran: Who has been killed?
item.description: A growing list of Iran's top leadership has been reportedly killed in ongoing strikes by Israel and the United States.

item.title: Crocodiles spotted in Newcastle creek
item.description: Reptile experts are searching for several juvenile freshwater crocodiles that have been spotted in a New South Wales creek.

item.title: Independent review of Inpex Ichthys LNG project finds emissions were 'systematically underestimated' for years
item.description: An independent review into oil and gas company Inpex has found widespread and persistent failures in how emissions were calculated, recorded and reported, dating back to the start of production in 2018.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death may not lead to regime change in Iran
item.description: The death of Iran's supreme leader leaves a power vacuum in a perilously unstable country. It could end with a military takeover and a new leader with a no-limits approach to war.

item.title: Iranians mourn death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Labor promises to wipe public school fees from 2027 while Liberals set target for zero litter in oceans within a decade
item.description: Labor has vowed to abolish public school fees for primary and high school students from 2027, if it's returned to Government, while the Liberal Party said it will commit $4.3 million to stop stormwater litter reaching South Australia's coastline.

item.title: Queensland talent scouts use 'world-first' AI to broaden search for 2032 Olympians
item.description: A sliding doors moment can be all it takes to put someone on the path to elite sport. But those in charge of finding our next Olympians have a "world-first" idea to change that.

item.title: Intercepted missile falls on neighbourhood in Doha
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Question about Khamenei's death to Iran's women's football team quickly shut down
item.description: As the world reacts to the death of Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Iranian women's football team prepares to play its opening Women's Asian Cup game in Australia.

item.title: Damage and chaos at Dubai International Airport after Iranian strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iranian drone strikes luxury apartment building in Bahrain
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ABC correspondent on the challenges of travelling to Israel
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sam Kerr fires Australia to victory over the Philippines in Asian Cup opener
item.description: The Matildas get off to a winning start, but the Filipinas don't make things easy in the opening game of the 2026 Women's Asian Cup at Perth Stadium.

item.title: Terror accused Jayson Joseph Michaels revealed as former private schoolboy and labourer
item.description: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, left school to undertake a TAFE course in IT and worked as a labourer in Bindoon, the ABC can reveal.

item.title: Protesters storm US consulate in Karachi.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Travellers sleep at airport amid warning of 'significant disruptions' from Middle East conflict
item.description: Australia's airports peak body has warned of "significant disruptions" to global aviation in coming days after the Middle East erupted into conflict and flights to the region were cancelled.

item.title: Demons AFL premiership defender Steven May retires ahead of 2026 season
item.description: Steven May, a key figure in the Demons' 2021 premiership win, announces his retirement just days out from the start of the AFL season.

item.title: Alyssa Healy scores century as Australia thrashes India by 185 runs in final ODI
item.description: Alyssa Healy puts an exclamation mark on her ODI career, as Australia records a 185-run win over India in Hobart.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: How the internet reacted to Matildas 1-0 win over Philippines
item.description: The Matildas opened their Women's Asian Cup 2026 campaign with a win, beating the Philippines 1-0 in Perth, and the internet watched on with bated breath.

item.title: Explosions rock Tehran as clerical regime vows to avenge Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: Outrage over the attack targeting Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei spilled over into neighbouring Iraq and Pakistan, where crowds attempted to storm American diplomatic missions.

item.title: Fires break out in Dubai, Sharjah after Iranian strikes on UAE
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Matthew Davey, who set ex-partner alight, charged with allegedly contacting her from prison
item.description: A Tasmanian man who was found guilty of attempting to murder his ex-partner has been charged with attempting to make her recant her evidence against him.

item.title: US jets hit by friendly fire, Israel issues evacuation orders in Lebanon — as it happened
item.description: US President Donald Trump says the US-Israeli operation in Iran could go on for four weeks.

item.title: Meet the Australians making the ultimate tree change
item.description: Moving to the country might sound idyllic to overstimulated and stressed-out city dwellers, but for Max and Courtney, it took some soul-searching to find their purpose and a place to really call home.

item.title: This week in history quiz, March 2: Can you pass the test?
item.description: Where was MH370 heading, which Australian won our first Oscar and what was the world's first vampire movie?

item.title: The four stages of the mental load explained
item.description: While research has found mothers do more in all four stages of cognitive labour, there was one stage where men were more active than others.

item.title: Tasmanian government spent more than $105 million last year to employ temporary nurses
item.description: State government spending on locum nurses continues to rapidly rise, despite repeated calls to build a more permanent workforce.

item.title: PM's swift support for US-Israel strikes in Iran shows how times have changed
item.description: The speed at which the Albanese government backed the US-Israel operation is a tale of a changed world and a radically altered domestic environment.

item.title: Melbourne woman fears for her mother in Iranian prison amid US-Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: As the US-Israel strikes ramp up, a Melbourne woman fears the Islamic Republic could execute her mother for a "false accusation" of "spying" on Israel.

item.title: How Marjorie Taylor Greene crossed Donald Trump to help unleash the Epstein files
item.description: Many of Donald Trump's supporters wanted to send those implicated in Jeffrey Epstein's sex trafficking ring "off to the gallows", but then the president changed his mind about releasing the files. What followed was an unusual political manoeuvre, acts of immense bravery by survivors and a backflip by the president of the United States.

item.title: American service members killed as Iran strikes back against US-Israel attack
item.description: As the US military confirms several deaths and injuries among its ranks, Donald Trump says the attack on Iran could be a "four-week process".

item.title: Interest rate hikes failing to dampen property prices in mid-size and regional areas
item.description: Nationally, the median house price for all the capital cities is now more than $1 million.

item.title: How one change to Disability Discrimination Act could make it less 'daunting'
item.description: Australia's Disability Discrimination Act is decades old, with reform "well overdue". Removing the burden of proof could be the first step.

item.title: Going once, going twice, sold! What's on offer this election from Labor and Liberals to fix the housing crisis
item.description: South Australia's housing market is running hot. When it comes to securing votes, the major parties want those caught up in the crisis to buy what they're selling.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Kerr makes difference, Fowler's class cameo and Lincoln cool in a crisis
item.description: Sam Kerr makes the difference, Mary Fowler has a class cameo and Chloe Lincoln is cool in an injury crisis. Here are the quick hits from the Matildas' Asian Cup opener against the Philippines.

item.title: What life looks like for Ukrainians who fled and can never return
item.description: As Russian forces took control of their cities, millions of Ukrainians fled. Many still live as refugees, fearing they may never see their families or home towns again.

item.title: When the Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix is on, how to watch, all F1 teams and drivers
item.description: New cars, new drivers and one entirely new team descend on Melbourne as Formula 1 returns for the 2026 season.

item.title: Bondi neighbourhood split over illegal chopping of tree in broad daylight
item.description: Residents of a quiet street in Bondi are split after men wearing high-vis and wielding chainsaws allegedly illegally destroyed a tree.

item.title: Diesel sales surge on home deliveries, while E10 and LPG sales plunge
item.description: New figures show Australian diesel sales are rising as customers buy more products online, while E10 and LPG sales continue to fall.

item.title: If Trump wants regime change, it will likely take more than killing Khamenei
item.description: Cracks are appearing in Iran's regime, but sparking the change Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu want to see will likely take more than killing the Ayatollah.

item.title: Matt Jacobs has a tick-induced red-meat allergy. Four doctors didn't know what it was
item.description: Australia's first death from a tick-induced allergy has sparked calls to ramp up awareness among the public and the medical profession.

item.title: Plea for government to help vulnerable kids as in-home childcare services face collapse
item.description: In-home care provides services to about 1,000 families, but its viability is under threat because it is excluded from a government payment covering rising worker costs.

item.title: As the price of intelligence collapses, agentic AI is replacing human workers
item.description: An entire retail and wholesale ecosystem has sprung from nothing in about a year, in a way that most people don't yet understand.

item.title: Playing before eating helps Central Coast public school students perform better in the classroom
item.description: Playing before eating has led to higher academic performance and fewer behavioural issues among students at a NSW public school.

item.title: US navy launches fighters and missiles in Operation Epic Fury
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: 'Messy' Matildas happy with win but promise more next time
item.description: The Matildas' 1-0 win over the Philippines in Perth wasn't their best performance. But they've promised better things are on the horizon — and there's reason to believe them.

item.title: India defeats West Indies, South Africa beats Zimbabwe in T20 World Cup Super Eights
item.description: India has reached the semifinals of the T20 World Cup with a five-wicket win over West Indies in Sunday's decisive Super Eights match.

item.title: Privacy fears as OpenAI trials ads in its ChatGPT chatbot in the US
item.description: The company said advertisements will appear at the bottom of answers and will be based on a user's current conversation.

item.title: Queensland on cyclone watch as rain drenches parts of state
item.description: Parts of Queensland are drenched after more than 200 millimetres of rain fell in less than 24 hours — and there is more rain and potentially a cyclone on the way.

item.title: ASX inches higher to record as Iran strikes rattle markets — as it happened
item.description: The ASX defies global anxieties to scamble back to marginal gain, aided by boom oil stocks, while banks tumbled

item.title: Smoke billows in Tehran’s night sky during US-Israel attacks
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aftermath of Iranian strike on Israel’s Beit Shemesh
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australians stranded due to airspace closures after US-Israel strikes on Iran
item.description: Australians are left stranded overseas amid ongoing hostilities in the Middle East, with one woman saying she was forced to sleep on an airport floor.

item.title: Terminally ill Mildura grandmother blasts regional airlines at Productivity Commission hearing
item.description: The Australian government asked this woman how she felt about regional airfares. She did not hold back.

item.title: Flash flooding hits Mildura after 100mm of rain in 24 hours
item.description: Heavy rainfall causes flash flooding in Mildura as the city records its wettest March day.

item.title: The Nightcliff by-election is this weekend. Here is why it's happening and how to vote
item.description: Residents living in Darwin's northern suburbs of Nightcliff, Rapid Creek and parts of Coconut Grove will be heading to the polls this Saturday to vote in a by-election. It comes after the former member for Nightcliff resigned in February.

item.title: Iran football chief pessimistic about FIFA World Cup following US air strikes
item.description: Reverberations from the Middle East conflict hits global sport, with Iran's soccer chief offering a bleak outlook for the FIFA World Cup in the United States.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at 2026 Eurovision
item.description: The Australian pop singer will perform her new single Eclipse at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna in May to a global audience of more than 160 million viewers.

item.title: 'Bittersweet' victory for traditional owners after mining damage to East Kimberley burial site
item.description: A sacred site in the remote East Kimberley has been declared an Aboriginal heritage protected area, years after burial grounds were damaged by a local mining company.

item.title: Winx colt withdrawn from sale after suspected 'paddock accident'
item.description: The son of the legendary racehorse had drawn global interest, after its sister sold for a record $10 million in 2024.

item.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears at alternative Adelaide Writers' Week event
item.description: Palestinian Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah has appeared at an alternative Adelaide Writers' Week event, telling a sold-out crowd journalists "muted" and "censored" Palestinian people amid the Gaza war.

item.title: Appeal for information about newborn baby boy left at Canberra fire station
item.description: The baby boy found at a south Canberra fire station in November last year is "safe" and "thriving" in foster care, as the ACT government issues a fresh appeal for information.

item.title: Albanese government avoids questions on legality of US-Israeli strikes on Iran
item.description: The Albanese government is continuing to avoid questions about the legality of the joint US-Israeli attacks on Iran, while throwing support behind the strikes.

item.title: NRL suspends Cowboys winger Braidon Burns after Las Vegas opener
item.description: North Queensland's Las Vegas hangover will extend well beyond a shock loss to Newcastle, with Braidon Burns receiving a two-match ban.

item.title: Crocodile spotted in suburban Newcastle creek captured
item.description: The sub-adult freshwater crocodile was first spotted on Saturday in a suburban creek. Reptile experts captured it on Sunday night and police are investigating how it came to be in Newcastle.

item.title: Josh Rachele, Nate Caddy and Jagga Smith headline breakout stars who could be 2026 AFL season's needle-movers
item.description: As the AFL season gets underway, each club is hoping for one of its young players to pop. Here's who is poised to take a leap in 2026.

item.title: Isaac council puts miner Vitrinite's $2.2m unpaid bill on hold amid collapse
item.description: Isaac Regional Council places a bill for "coal haulage charges" totalling more than $2 million on hold, as administrators work to meet existing obligations.

item.title: Lawyers for NRL player Zac Lomax and Parramatta Eels continue last-minute negotiations over breakup
item.description: Zac Lomax's NRL future remains in limbo as his lawyers conduct last-minute talks to secure his release from Parramatta.

item.title: Satellite images from Iran show destroyed buildings, impact craters, submerged ships
item.description: Satellite imagery is giving the world some insight into the scale of destruction inside Iran and across the region after a weekend of heavy military bombardments by the US, Israel and Iran.

item.title: Wilcannia Weir project delayed for decades gets delayed again by funding shortfall
item.description: An outback community on the Darling River has been calling for a new town weir for decades. After numerous delays, the Wilcannia Weir has hit another hurtle with the state government blaming the federal government for the funding shortfall.

item.title: Daniil Medvedev among tennis stars stranded in Dubai after air strikes, Zimbabwe cricketers stuck in India
item.description: Daniil Medvedev says he is unsure when he will be able to leave Dubai after the exchange of air strikes between the US, Israel and Iran shut down the UAE airport.

item.title: Actor Awards winners list: Sinners soars, Catherine O'Hara honoured and remembered
item.description: The Studio co-creator Seth Rogen accepted the award on O'Hara's behalf, following the death of the comedy legend in January.

item.title: Motorcyclist who died during SA downpour was on memorial ride for his dad
item.description: An outback tragedy has claimed the life of avid motorcyclist Darran Hyman, whose death during SA's weekend wet weather came almost exactly two years after his own father was also killed during a ride.

item.title: Former patients of gynaecologist Simon Gordon not included in Epworth Hospital review
item.description: Women who had surgery for "severe" endometriosis despite pathology showing little trace of the disease are angry and upset that Epworth Hospital has not invited them to participate in an external review.

item.title: Formula 1 Australian Grand Prix to run without issue, CEO says, despite Middle East conflict disrupting travel
item.description: Formula 1's season opener is expected to go ahead without a hitch this weekend in Melbourne, after conflict in the Middle East forced a change in travel plans for many teams.

item.title: Iranian diaspora in London celebrates the death of Ayatollah Khamenei
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Canberra man accused of sexually assaulting woman he held captive found guilty
item.description: A man accused of holding a woman captive until she fled naked into a Canberra street is found guilty in the ACT Supreme Court.

item.title: At least 150 reportedly killed after strike on Iranian school
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran claims US and Israel attacked a girls' school. This is what is known about the strike
item.description: Iran says the death toll from an attack on a girls' school it claims was launched by the US and Israel has surpassed 165.

item.title: 'Cyclonic' dust storm blankets northern WA town of Onslow
item.description: A photographer from a coastal mining town in WA described the "cyclonic" winds as a thick dust storm blanketed her town.

item.title: Strong wind gusts lashed the town of Onslow in dust.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran's next supreme leader may have been waiting for this moment to rise
item.description: Behind the scenes of Iran's clerical authoritarian regime, cogs have been churning for years, if not decades, to ensure its survival in the event it was decapitated. While Ali Khamanei's followers mourn his death, a plan to replace him is already being enacted.

item.title: NSW health minister rejects 'negligence' claims after snap review into hospitals
item.description: The review comes after a fungal infection cluster at a transplant ward at Sydney's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital linked to two deaths.

item.title: ARL Commission chair Peter V'landys says NRL's Las Vegas round will not lose its attraction
item.description: ARL Commission chair Peter V'Landys is adamant Las Vegas can remain a success beyond 2027 and insists fans will continue turning up once their team returns for a second time.

item.title: Vision from US Central Command of strikes on Iranian facilities
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dairy farmers want plant-based drinks to stop using the word 'milk'. But does it really confuse consumers?
item.description: Australian dairy farmers say the plant-based drinks industry has "stolen" the word milk, and they are pointing to a recent court ruling as an opportunity to claim it back.

item.title: IBAC apologises to family violence victim for mishandling complaints about Victoria Police
item.description: IBAC's public apology to Jana Katerinskaja is a sharp reminder of the struggle for accountability victims of police-perpetrated family violence too often face.

item.title: Jessica Zrinski inquest told details of man believed to be last to see her alive
item.description: An inquest has heard Sydney woman Jessica Zrinski went missing in November 2022 after meeting a man she did not know in a pub car park.

item.title: Canberra teen accused of driving stolen car during fatal crash tells court he was left 'hanging from the seatbelt'
item.description: A teenager accused of being the driver in a fatal crash outside the prime minister's home in Canberra gives a dramatic account of how he was left hanging upside down in the car.

item.title: David Connolly speaks on Mix FM this morning after his swearing-in on Friday.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NT Administrator David Connolly sends mixed message on social media posts
item.description: New NT Administrator David Connolly has extended invitations to groups which say they will not work with him, in an attempt to repair divisions caused by controversial past social media posts.

item.title: Emergency declaration in place as authorities monitor flood risk in Alice Springs
item.description: An emergency has been declared as as a tropical low brings heavy rain, rising river levels and flooding risks to Central Australia and the Barkly region.

item.title: NDIS regional support services boosted by FIFO healthcare program
item.description: A joint state and federal government program will deploy fly-in fly-out allied healthcare workers to reduce geographical impacts on regional residents accessing disability support.

item.title: Five of our best autumn recipes
item.description: We’ve crunched the numbers and these are five of your favourite ever autumn recipes to look forward to this season.

item.title: Former teacher Stephen Fane Noga's letters to former student shown to court at abuse trial
item.description: A former teacher accused of sexually abusing a student has told a court he is "ashamed of my behaviour at that time" and says he was "temporarily blinded".

item.title: Coronial inquest begins into death of man who fell on zip-line tour in Far North Queensland
item.description: A coronial inquest into the 2019 death of 50-year-old Dean Sanderson has heard the mining project manager was visiting Far North Queensland with his wife, after "a pretty terrible health situation".

item.title: Economists warn Middle East war to hit households through oil price spike but say fallout could be short-lived
item.description: The average household could see $14 added to their weekly petrol bill, a prominent economist has warned. While oil prices have already spiked, analysts are hopeful the conflict will be short-lived, limiting the fallout.

item.title: Liberals give conditional support to free school plan, as Labor pledges OSHC expansion
item.description: Labor has announced a plan for further funding to expand out of school hours care, while the Liberal Party now supports the concept of free schools and is demanding a freight bypass to get trucks off city roads.

item.title: Tasmania's largest lamb exporter loses biggest market in the wake of Iran war
item.description: A meat exporter among those caught in the fallout of the US-Israeli bombing of Iran says his business will have to freeze hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of produce.

item.title: Hanson censured over no 'good' Muslims comment, Coalition votes against
item.description: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.

item.title: Father 'prolonged suffering' of son by delaying medical help, court hears
item.description: The SA Supreme Court hears a father failed to get his son medical help for 16 hours before he died. WARNING: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander readers are advised that this article contains the name of a person who has died, as well as details that may be distressing.

item.title: Former Riverina gym teacher jailed for historical child sex offences
item.description: A man who taught gymnastics at an all-boys Catholic school in the Riverina has been sentenced to three years in prison for historical child sex offences.

item.title: Australian memorials for Iran's former leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei condemned
item.description: The move to hold memorials and prayer sessions for Iran's former leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is slammed by the NSW premier, Iranian Australians and anti-regime activists.

item.title: Brisbane house prices hit new peak as national market growth continues
item.description: The average home in Brisbane is now worth $1,046,000 — an increase of 15.9 per cent since the same time last year, according to data from Proptrack.

item.title: NSW government grants six-year extension to controversial Dartbrook coal mine
item.description: The NSW government grants a six-year extension to a controversial coal mine in the Hunter Valley.

item.title: Middle East airspace closure could leave Australians trapped for weeks after US-Israel strikes on Iran
item.description: An aviation expert says it could be weeks before flights resume in and out of the Middle East once airspace reopens, following strikes on Iran.

item.title: Not obvious sexual assault complainant was a child, ex-MP Rory Amon tells court
item.description: According to the Crown's case, the teenager was sexually and indecently assaulted in the toilet of an underground car park during two encounters in mid-2017.

item.title: AFL chief Andrew Dillon defends Opening Round and says State of Origin will evolve but stay
item.description: AFL chief Andrew Dillon backs the contentious Opening Round format and says State of Origin matches are here to stay.

item.title: Fighter jet crashes in Kuwait near the city of Al Jahra
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Coalition brands Australian children in Syria terrorist 'sympathisers'
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has escalated his attack on the federal government over its response to the so-called "ISIS brides" cohort.

item.title: The IDF released footage of a bombing in Tabriz
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Video shows damage at a hospital in Tehran's Ghandi Street area
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dubai International Airport was damaged in a strike by Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Era Tower in Manama, Bahrain
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Projectile fragment falling in Qatar
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: The Fairmont Hotel in Dubai
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Weekend of war: Key moments from the first 48 hours of strikes in the Middle East
item.description: As the war in the Middle East escalates, ABC NEWS Verify has gathered verified video and satellite imagery in an early attempt to piece together some of the key events of the past 48 hours.

item.title: Backlash deepens over AI seatbelt cameras as drivers fined for passengers’ behaviour
item.description: Frustration is deepening over drivers being ordered to pay hundreds of dollars in fines for infringements incurred by their passengers — including young children and neurodivergent people not wearing seatbelts correctly.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Inquest hears Krista Kach threw axe at police before being shot with bean-bag round
item.description: An inquest has been told a Newcastle woman who died after being shot with a bean-bag round, threw an axe at police when they entered her home.

item.title: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese condemns mourning of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in Australia
item.description: Anthony Albanese says mourning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei is not appropriate on Australian soil as he sidesteps a big question on Donald Trump's actions.

item.title: Hawthorn co-captain Jai Newcombe gets one-week ban for rough conduct, Hawks to appeal
item.description: Gun midfielder Jai Newcombe will challenge his one-match ban for rough conduct in a bid to play in Hawthorn's season opener against GWS.

item.title: Middle East in turmoil
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Conflict widens in the Middle East
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iran beaten in opening game of Women's AFC Asian Cup as tensions escalate back home
item.description: Playing despite heightened tensions back home, Iran were thrashed 3-0 by 2022 runners up South Korea on the Gold Coast in their opening match of the tournament ahead of Thursday's game against the Matildas.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap narrows but gender segregated industries persist, WGEA finds
item.description: Is your employer among the largest or smallest gender pay gaps? More than 5.9 million workers from over 10,000 employers can now see the data for themselves.

item.title: Live: Trump says 'big wave' of US-Israel attacks on Iran 'coming soon'
item.description: The United States and Israel are continuing to launch strikes at Iran as Tehran becomes increasingly isolated after attacking neighbouring countries. Follow live.
